_comment: BASE CASE 1 of 2 — Coinbase. Demonstrates CREATIVE PUBLIC-SOURCE COLLECTION (every assertion is REAL & citable).
_role_in_demo: The 'breadth of sources' showcase: SEC/EDGAR filings, GLEIF, OFAC, regulators (BaFin/MiCA), on-chain, news. Not the silent LOW->HIGH drift hero (that's the ADGM startup) — Coinbase onboards already-elevated; its drift is continuous profile/regulatory expansion.
_real_vs_simulated: Identity, business, ownership and regulatory facts are REAL & publicly sourced (URLs given; mark VERIFY on exact IDs/figures). The bank-side risk_score, review cadence, and the 'as an AMINA institutional client' framing are SIMULATED — illustrative only.
_structure_note: entity_profile = static identity (not drift-monitored). assertions = the dated, sourced beliefs the engine re-validates.
purpose_of_relationship: Institutional custody & treasury for a listed digital-asset exchange group (SIMULATED onboarding rationale).
_open_question: Coinbase is illustrative of a Coinbase-LIKE institutional client; state this on stage so we don't imply a real banking relationship.

risk_model._note: A regulated crypto exchange onboards ELEVATED, not LOW. Drift here = upward pressure from regulatory actions + global expansion, not a tier flip.

kyc_review._note: Risk-based periodic review. MEDIUM band -> 24-month cadence.
kyc_review._derivation: MEDIUM -> 24mo -> 2021-04-14 + 24mo = 2023-04-14. Regulatory escalation (SEC suit 2023) should have pulled this off-cycle — a point the demo can make.

entity_profile._note: Static KYC identity block — collected at onboarding, not monitored for drift.
entity_profile.registered_address: Delaware, US (remote-first; no central HQ since 2020)
entity_profile.regulated_financial_entity: Partially — NYDFS BitLicense, multiple US MTLs, EU MiCA & BaFin (VERIFY current scope)

assertions.notes: REAL. Drifts via continuous expansion into staking, derivatives (Deribit acq. 2025), stablecoins (USDC), L2 (Base), payments.
REAL — first major crypto exchange to list on a US exchange.

assertions.value: {"Brian Armstrong": "co-founder/CEO — majority voting via Class B (20 votes/share)", "Fred Ehrsam": "co-founder/director"}
assertions.source: Coinbase proxy statement (DEF 14A) / S-1 — dual-class structure
assertions.notes: REAL — dual-class (Class A 1 vote / Class B 20 votes) concentrates voting control in founders. A genuine UBO/control nuance for a listed entity.
REAL — international footprint widened post-listing (EU MiCA, Ireland, Germany, etc.).

assertions.value: NYDFS BitLicense + US MTLs; later EU MiCA & BaFin crypto-custody licence
assertions.notes: REAL — regulatory perimeter is itself a moving target (drift signal). VERIFY current licence list.

assertions.value: No current sanctions designation (OFAC / EU / UN); one historic OFAC civil settlement (2020, sanctions-screening lapses), resolved
assertions.notes: REAL — a settled compliance finding, NOT a designation. Good example of nuanced screening (a hit that needs human judgement, not auto-escalation). VERIFY exact figures.

assertions.value: Material litigation exposure — SEC v. Coinbase (filed 2023)
assertions.source: SEC litigation release / court docket / press
assertions.notes: REAL — SEC suit filed Jun 2023, later dropped (2025). The clean->litigation->resolved arc is a strong adverse-media DRIFT demonstration over the relationship lifetime.

assertions.value: Holds BTC, ETH and other crypto on the corporate balance sheet (custodial + proprietary)
assertions.notes: REAL — relevant for an AMINA (crypto-bank) client; proof-of-reserves style disclosures are public.

assertions.value: Transaction fees + subscription & services (custody, staking, interest income)
assertions.notes: REAL — revenue mix shifted toward subscription & services over time (another slow-drift signal).
SIMULATED. 60 = top of MEDIUM band. For Coinbase the demo point is sourcing breadth + steady upward pressure (regulatory actions), not a dramatic LOW->HIGH flip.
